Ad Text:
Senior Technical Business Analyst (2 Roles Available)

Initial 6‑month contract + 6‑month extension option
Hybrid working - ACT based (3 days onsite, 2 days from home)
Full time - 37.5 hours per week
Security clearance not required

We are seeking two experienced Senior Technical Business Analysts to join a high‑performing team supporting large‑scale data, cloud and analytics initiatives within the Australian public sector.

This role is ideal for candidates who enjoy working in complex environments, collaborating closely with both technical and policy stakeholders, and helping shape future‑state systems and services through well‑defined processes and requirements.

The successful candidates will work in a fast‑paced environment, contributing to the design and delivery of high‑quality data and analytical solutions that inform Australia's health and welfare decision‑making.

Key Responsibilities
1. Business Process Documentation and Cloud Integration Analysis

* Document complex and routine business processes using BPMN, flowcharts and structured templates
* Analyse current‑ and future‑state workflows to identify integration points and alignment with cloud and analytics architectures
* Work with modern cloud and data platforms

2. Requirements Definition and Future‑State Platform Design

* Conduct gap analysis and translate business needs into actionable, prioritised requirements
* Document future‑state business processes, systems and services aligned with enterprise data and analytics strategies

3. Stakeholder Engagement and Collaboration

* Partner with policy, operational, cloud and technical stakeholders
* Build consensus, support co‑design practices and facilitate alignment across diverse groups

4. Data, Analytics and Cloud Platform Capability

* Perform data analysis and visualisation using tools such as AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, SAS, Power BI or Tableau
* Understand cloud‑based data and analytics concepts relevant to public sector reporting and insights

5. Business Case Development and Organisational Support

* Develop evidence‑based business cases supported by data, cost‑benefit analysis and risk assessment
* Support business units with varying technical maturity, including identifying capability and training needs

6. Interpersonal Effectiveness and Responsiveness

* Build strong working relationships and maintain trust across stakeholder groups
* Demonstrate active listening, regular check‑ins and a flexible approach to evolving priorities

Working Arrangements

* Location: ACT
* Onsite: Three days per week, two days from home
* Hours: 37.5 hours per week
* Experience level: Senior (EL1 equivalent)
* Security clearance: Not required
* Estimated start date: Monday, 23 March 2026

About the Organisation

This organisation provides reliable, national data and analysis across the health, housing and community services sectors. Their work supports national insights into expenditure, hospitals, disability, mental health, ageing, vulnerable populations, homelessness, Indigenous health and more.

They pride themselves on an inclusive, respectful and high‑performing culture, consistently achieving top‑ten rankings for engagement and wellbeing in the APS Employee Census.

Consultant

Change and Communication Specialist

Contract | 6 months + 6‑month extension option
Location: Canberra ACT
Hybrid: 3 days in office, 2 days from home
Hours: Up to 37.5 per week
Clearance: Not required
Level: EL1 equivalent

A major Government agency is seeking an experienced Change and Communication Specialist to support the successful delivery of a significant IT and business transformation initiative. This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated professional who thrives in fast‑paced, high‑performance environments and enjoys translating complex technical change into clear, meaningful outcomes for stakeholders.

About the Role

You will play a key role in designing and delivering targeted communication and change activities that support adoption of new systems, platforms, and processes. Working closely with policy, operational, and technical teams, you will ensure stakeholders understand impacts, are prepared for change, and are supported throughout delivery.

This role requires strong judgement, exceptional stakeholder engagement skills, and the ability to operate with professionalism and flexibility in a dynamic program environment.

Key Responsibilities
1. Communication and Change Delivery (IT and Analytics Initiatives)

* Design and deliver communication and change management activities for IT, data, and analytics projects
* Apply structured change methodologies to assess impacts and support adoption
* Support delivery teams to ensure smooth implementation and user readiness

2. Governance and Executive Support

* Coordinate program inputs, timelines, and milestones to support governance
* Prepare high‑quality executive and board‑level briefings, papers, and presentations
* Ensure alignment of schedules, dependencies, and reporting requirements

3. Stakeholder Engagement

* Build strong relationships across leadership, technical teams, and business areas
* Translate complex technical concepts into accessible messaging for diverse audiences
* Facilitate shared understanding of impacts, benefits, and required actions

4. Training and Capability Uplift

* Develop and coordinate training materials and capability uplift activities
* Identify skills and workforce readiness needs arising from change
* Support business areas to prepare staff for new processes and systems

5. Integration with Delivery Teams

* Work closely with project and program teams to align change and communication activities
* Apply pragmatic and adaptable approaches in a fast‑paced environment

6. Interpersonal Effectiveness

* Build trust and positive working relationships with a wide stakeholder base
* Maintain high quality outcomes while adapting to shifting priorities

About the Organisation

This Government agency provides trusted national data, insights, and analysis across Australia's health, housing, and welfare sectors. The organisation values collaboration, flexibility, and excellence, and consistently ranks highly for employee engagement and wellbeing.

Staff enjoy modern technology, flexible working arrangements, strong professional development support, and an inclusive, respectful workplace culture. The organisation actively welcomes applications from people of all backgrounds, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with disability, LGBTIQA+ individuals, neurodiverse applicants, and culturally and linguistically diverse communities.

About the Company

Join a dynamic, purpose-driven organisation supporting innovation, creativity, and community impact across Victoria. The team is leading a major digital transformation to modernise how programs, partnerships, and stakeholder engagement are managed. This includes the implementation of a new CRM platform to improve how the organisation engages with stakeholders, manages programs, and handles data.




The Opportunity

We are seeking a Data Migration Analyst/ Business Analyst to support the final stages of this transformation, with a strong focus on data migration and data readiness.

This role will work closely with the project team and Data Analyst to understand how system changes impact data structures, fields, and migration templates. You will act as the bridge between stakeholders, vendors, and the technical team, ensuring the right information is gathered and translated so the data migration process can progress efficiently.

It’s a role suited to someone who enjoys untangling complexity, working with data models, and bringing clarity to evolving systems and requirements.




What You'll Do

This role will focus on ensuring the data required for the new platform is clearly defined, accurately mapped, and aligned with the evolving system configuration. You will gather information from multiple sources, identify how changes impact data structures, and support the Data Analyst by ensuring the right context and inputs are available for migration activities.

Analyse the new platform to identify required fields and data structures
Review and validate migration templates against system configuration
Identify how changes to functionality impact related data fields and tables
Gather information from vendors, stakeholders, and project documentation
Translate system changes into clear requirements for data migration
Work closely with the Data Analyst to ensure data can be sourced and loaded correctly
Support the project team in identifying bugs or changes that impact data
Maintain clear documentation around field definitions, data sources, and mappings



What You'll Bring

You are someone who enjoys working with complex data environments and can bring structure and clarity when information is coming from multiple sources. You are comfortable translating technical concepts into clear requirements and ensuring others have the context they need to deliver their work effectively.

You will also be someone who communicates clearly, organises information logically, and can create structured inputs that allow technical specialists to focus on execution.

Experience as a Business Analyst or Data Analyst within system implementation projects
Strong understanding of data models, field mapping, and data migration concepts
Experience working with CRM platforms, ideally Microsoft Dynamics 365
Ability to analyse how system changes impact data structures and relationships
Strong attention to detail and ability to manage complex information sets
Clear and structured communication style, able to document and organise requirements
Comfortable working closely with technical specialists and supporting their workflow
Ability to gather and synthesise information from vendors, stakeholders, and project teams



Not sure if you meet these requirements? Please apply - we are happy to discuss your individual circumstances.

Multiple 6 + 6 month contracts, $$$ negotiable
Canberra, Bruce, + 2 days WFH
Baseline security clearance preferred

This opportunity sits within a Commonwealth Government policy team working on international education and regional engagement. The team focuses on strengthening Australia’s education partnerships across Southeast Asia and supporting Australian providers to expand their education offerings offshore.
 
The work sits within a collaborative policy environment that engages closely with government agencies, industry stakeholders and the higher education sector to support Australia’s broader regional engagement objectives.
 
The Opportunity

An opportunity is available for an APS6 Researcher/Policy Officer to support a major policy project focused on the development of an educational initiative for Southeast Asia.
 
This project will contribute to broader government work supporting Australian education providers to expand their presence across Southeast Asia and deliver education internationally. The role will support the research and evidence base underpinning the roadmap, helping inform policy development and strategic planning.
 
Working closely with a small policy team, the position will involve analysing policy material, synthesising insights from multiple sources and supporting consultation with stakeholders across government, industry and the higher education sector.
 
The Role

Key responsibilities of the role may include:

Conduct research and analysis of policy documents and open-source material to inform project development and strategic planning
Analyse and synthesise information from multiple sources to support evidence-based policy outcomes
Present findings and insights clearly for both technical and non-technical audiences
Support stakeholder engagement activities across government, industry and the higher education sector
Assist with consultation activities and provide secretariat support where required
Prepare policy and project documentation including briefs, reports, correspondence, talking points and background material
Contribute to monitoring, evaluation and other project support activities
The Ideal Person

Job Title: Commercial Finance Manager - PE Backed Tech/SaaS - 6 month contract
Ad Text:
Allura Partners are delighted to be partnering with a PE backed SaaS business on a search for a Commercial Finance Manager. Reporting to the Head of Commercial Finance, this role partners with Sales, Operations, Delivery and Product leaders to deliver commercial insight across the largest business division. The position turns operational and commercial data into actionable insights, improving forecasting discipline, margin visibility and strategic decision making.

Please note, we need a candidate immediately available or on very short notice.

Benefits:
Competitive remuneration aligned with a senior commercial finance role
Exposure to senior leadership and strategic decision making
Hybrid working environment based in Sydney
Opportunity to influence revenue growth, retention and margin performance
Work within a high growth technology-driven business
Duties / Key responsibilities:

Proven ability to influence senior stakeholders and translate complex data into clear insights

Job Title: BOS P&C SME and Business Lead
Category:
Consulting & Generalist HR (Human Resources & Recruitment)
Other (Education & Training)
Ad Text:
Employment Type: Full Time, Continuing role
Remuneration: Total Remuneration Package
Location: Kensington, NSW – Hybrid Work Profile

About UNSW

At UNSW, we pride ourselves on being a workplace where the best people come to do their best work.  We aspire to be Australia’s global university, improving and transforming lives through excellence in research, outstanding education and a commitment to advancing a just society. 

About the Role

The Business Optimisation Strategy (BOS) is a landmark investment to modernise UNSW’s platforms and simplify service delivery. By streamlining processes and data, BOS enables the "Progress for All" strategy, returning time to staff for teaching and research.

As BOS mobilises, we seek a senior leader with deep expertise in People & Culture service delivery, employment legislation, and enterprise agreements. Reporting to the P&C Program Manager, this role is the accountable expert for ERP and Service Management design, ensuring workforce systems are human-centred, compliant, and efficient.

You will influence program direction, lead HR transformation elements, and ensure the future operating model meets the needs of the UNSW community. This is a rare opportunity to define the employee experience and systems that will underpin talent attraction, payroll, and retention for the next decade. This role is not a project management or business analyst opportunity.

Responsibilities

Serve as the senior People & Culture voice within the BOS program, ensuring all design decisions reflect the P&C service lifecycle and future operating model.
Embed employment legislation, wage compliance, and enterprise agreement interpretation into all system configurations and regulatory requirements.
Drive the end-to-end design and delivery of ERP and Service Management capabilities, including Payroll, Recruitment, Talent Management, and Workforce Planning.
Analyze current PeopleSoft configurations and integrations to define and transition to the future state within Oracle Cloud HCM.
Support workstream planning, dependency mapping, and governance reporting in alignment with central program office and EPMO standards.
Collaborate with academic and professional leaders to build consensus on delivery approaches, risk treatments, and P&C service workflows.
Partner with multidisciplinary teams and change management to ensure system integrations, business value, and university-wide adoption are sustained beyond go-live.

About You

Essential: At least 10 years of experience delivering enterprise-wide People & Culture solutions and large-scale ERP transformations, specifically centered on Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM and ServiceNow.
Tertiary qualifications in a relevant discipline or equivalent expertise gained through executive training and hands-on program leadership in ERP and HCM domains.
Deep expertise in ERP HCM solution design, configuration, and lifecycle management, with a track record of delivering Core HR, Job Architecture, Payroll, Time & Attendance, Rostering & Scheduling, Talent Acquisition, Learning & Development, Workforce Planning, and Performance & Career frameworks. Experience in higher education, healthcare or similarly federated and regulated sectors is desirable.
Proven capability to lead and inspire high-performing, cross-functional teams across business and technology domains to ensure delivery excellence.
Strong engagement and influencing skills, with a record of engaging senior executives and distilling complex ERP data into actionable insights and briefings.
Proven ability to navigate technical complexity and manage dependencies, risks, and trade-offs under conditions of ambiguity and high scrutiny.
Practical knowledge of PeopleSoft HR modules and a commitment to UNSW’s values to drive human-centered change and organizational agility.

For further information on the role & responsibilities, please refer to the Position Description.

How to Apply: Submit your CV & cover letter detailing your interest and suitability for the job (as per the skills & experience bullet points in the job description) before Sunday 22nd March 2026 by 11:55pm.



Please note: Sponsorship is not available for this role, valid Australian working rights are required on application.

 

UNSW Benefits and Culture: People are at the core of everything we do. We recognise it is the contributions of our staff who make UNSW one of the best universities in Australia and the world. Our benefits include: 

Additional 3 days leave during December festive period.
Career development opportunities 
Up to 50% discount on UNSW courses
Flexible 17% Superannuation contributions, additional leave-loading payments and salary sacrifice.
Discounts and entitlements (retail, education, fitness passport)

Job queries contact: Tai James, Talent Acquisition Consultant

E: tai.james@unsw.edu.au  (applications to the address cannot be accepted, please apply online)

We believe technology alone doesn't solve problems, people do. Our consultants sit at the intersection of strategy and delivery, working with senior leaders to close the gap between AI ambition and real outcomes.

We're growing our consulting team and looking for multiple AI Consultants across mid and senior levels.

The role

You'll work with senior leaders at large to medium organisations to shape their AI strategy, build the case for change, and guide them through adoption. That means facilitating workshops, developing roadmaps, managing complex stakeholders and working closely with our engineering teams to make sure strategy connects to delivery.

You won't be producing reports for a drawer. You'll be accountable for outcomes.

What we're looking for

Deep AI experience isn't the expectation, the field is too new and moving too fast. What matters is that you're a strong consultant or domain expert who is genuinely curious about AI and motivated to build expertise quickly. We'll back you with frameworks, methodology and a team that's been doing this in production.

You'll come from a background in management consulting, IT transformation and change management, or a senior role in mining, finance or government. You're comfortable presenting to a CTO or CIO, you can structure a problem clearly, and you don't lose momentum when things are ambiguous.

If AI is something you're actively thinking about, reading about and exploring, that is a positive.

Why Horizon Digital

We are at the leading edge of AI implementation, so we have the opportunity to really impact organisations that are just beginning to leverage the power of AI.

We move fast, we deliver, and we're building something worth being part of.
